description_long: This object is a row of six attached single-story houses, each featuring a gable roof with a muted reddish-brown color. The front of each house has a small porch with steps leading up to the entryway, flanked by green lawns and paved walkways. The facades are painted in a light beige or cream color with red accents around the windows and doors, giving a uniform and orderly appearance. Windows are symmetrically arranged on the front and rear sides, with each unit having two front-facing windows and a central door. The rear of the houses features similar window arrangements and small backyard areas. The overall structure is compact and linear, typical of suburban or urban row housing developments.
description: A row of six attached single-story houses with gable roofs, front porches, and symmetrical window arrangements. The facades are beige with red accents and each unit has a small lawn and steps leading to the entryway.
description_view_0: Front view showing all six house entrances, porches, steps, and green lawns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the row houses and the repeating gable roof structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back windows and small backyard areas of each house.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the length of the row and the rear lawns.
attribute_entryway_design: Central door with small porch, steps, and overhanging roof for each unit
